architecture_fit.note: Clean minimal delta from base_model.py (embedding + 1 causal RoPE head, MLP dropped) and hits ρ=1.0, but swaps the canonical pre-trained GPT-2 head for a frozen random head, so the result is near-tautological for the goal's intent.

baseline_comparison.note: Two strawmen fail clearly: framework random-attention baseline (ρ=0.13, flat) and content-ablated position-only head (ρ=0), both well below the full head's ρ=1.0.

faithfulness.note: Content-pathway ablation knocks token identity out, attention becomes token-independent, distance collapses to ~0 and correlation vanishes — exactly the right causal knockout, run through the same evaluate path.

operating_range.note: Holds (ρ≈0.97-1.0) across vocab 20→1000 (~1.7 orders), seq 8→128, edits 0→16, transparently per-regime; just shy of a full 2 orders and never shows a true breaking point.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Explicitly hand-built: frozen random-init weights, never trained, mechanism written out by hand — exactly what this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Demo leads with the 3-curve ablation plot (full vs content-KO vs baseline) then a per-regime operating-range panel with a ρ bar chart — legible and makes the claim at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties axes to the benchmark (y = 1−cosine, x = Levenshtein), justifies the ablation as the smallest flip-the-claim artefact and the range panel as the robustness check.

notes: A hand-built frozen single-head circuit that recovers a perfectly monotonic edit-distance/attention-distance curve (ρ=1.0) with a clean content-pathway ablation and a random-attention baseline, plus broad operating-range coverage. The main weakness is architecture fit: it substitutes a synthetic random head for the canonical GPT-2 head, making the monotonicity somewhat tautological rather than a claim about a real model's syntactic tracking. Strong on causal evidence, baselines, and visualisation, earning a solid 'good' (mean ≈4.7).
